details: The scheme “Uttarakhand@25 Adarsh Champawat” is implemented by the Uttarakhand State Council for Science and Technology (UCOST) under the Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand. It aims to develop Champawat district as a model district with the broader objective of positioning Uttarakhand as a model state for other Himalayan regions.
benefits: Uttarakhand@25 "Adarsh Champawat" aims to develop Champawat district as a model district under the vision of the Honorable Chief Minister, with the broader goal of making Uttarakhand a model for other Himalayan states. The Uttarakhand State Council for Science and Technology (UCOST), Dehradun, has been designated as the nodal agency. In this context, UCOST is working to develop the district as an ideal district by coordinating with NGOs, self-help groups, various state and central government departments, and other institutions operating in Champawat district.
eligibility: The beneficiaries must be farmers, entrepreneurs, self-help groups, students, researchers, and the general public of Champawat district of Uttarakhand.

offline: The selection of beneficiaries will be facilitated by constructing various complexes across all four blocks of Champawat district.

documents_required: Documents are required to be submitted as per the guidelines of the Uttarakhand State Council for Science and Technology (UCOST).

faqs.question: Who implements the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is implemented by the Uttarakhand State Council for Science and Technology (UCOST) under the Department of Science and Technology, Government of Uttarakhand.

faqs.question: What is the primary objective of the scheme?
faqs.answer: Its primary objective is to develop Champawat as an ideal district through coordination with NGOs, self-help groups, line departments, and central institutions.

faqs.question: Who is the nodal agency for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The Uttarakhand State Council for Science and Technology (UCOST), Dehradun, has been designated as the nodal agency.

faqs.question: Who can benefit from the scheme?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries include farmers, entrepreneurs, self-help groups, students, researchers, and the general public of Champawat district.

faqs.question: How are beneficiaries selected?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries are selected through the establishment of various complexes across all four blocks of Champawat district.
